name: Cube / Box
description: An axis-aligned cube or rectangular box, sometimes described as a cube within a sphere. Rare but consistently reported in sensor-era military accounts. Ryan Graves described encounters where the object was described as a 'box flying into a 120-knot headwind.' Some accounts include a translucent sphere surrounding the cube. Perfect axial lock — does not rotate or yaw. May relate to the pyramid-shaped objects in USS Russell footage.

ir_signature.ir_notes: USS Russell footage (2019) shows pyramid shapes with triangular-IR-signature possibly from aperture diffraction. True cube cases are mostly verbal accounts.

cultural_context: Cube/box UAPs are the most geometrically dissonant — nothing in aeronautics is a cube. The perfect right-angle aesthetics and axial-lock behavior suggest something that doesn't need aerodynamic form. ISS footage occasionally shows box-shaped objects (usually thermal blankets or antenna covers) feeding the visual canon. The cube-in-sphere variant from pilot accounts is particularly striking as a design object.
